effect: Increased bleeding risk due to combined anticoagulant and antiplatelet effects
recommendation: Avoid combination unless specifically indicated by cardiologist. If used together, monitor INR closely and watch for signs of bleeding.

effect: NSAIDs increase bleeding risk with warfarin and can cause GI hemorrhage
recommendation: Avoid NSAIDs with warfarin. Use acetaminophen for pain instead. If NSAID essential, add PPI and monitor INR.

effect: Some antibiotics can increase warfarin effect by disrupting gut flora vitamin K production
recommendation: Monitor INR more frequently during antibiotic course. Adjust warfarin dose if needed.

effect: Increased risk of lactic acidosis, hypoglycemia
recommendation: Limit alcohol intake. Avoid binge drinking. Monitor blood glucose closely.

effect: Risk of contrast-induced nephropathy and lactic acidosis
recommendation: Hold metformin 48h before and after contrast procedures. Check renal function before restarting.

effect: Hyperkalemia risk — ACE inhibitors reduce potassium excretion
recommendation: Avoid potassium supplements unless documented hypokalemia. Monitor serum potassium regularly.

effect: NSAIDs reduce antihypertensive effect and increase renal impairment risk
recommendation: Use lowest NSAID dose for shortest duration. Monitor BP and renal function.

effect: Dual RAAS blockade increases hyperkalemia, hypotension, and renal failure risk
recommendation: Do not combine ACE inhibitors with ARBs. Choose one class.

effect: Clarithromycin inhibits CYP3A4, dramatically increasing statin levels — rhabdomyolysis risk
recommendation: Suspend statin during clarithromycin course. Use azithromycin as alternative antibiotic if possible.

effect: Grapefruit inhibits CYP3A4, increasing statin blood levels and side effect risk
recommendation: Limit grapefruit consumption. Monitor for muscle pain, weakness.

effect: Serotonin syndrome — potentially fatal. Hyperthermia, rigidity, myoclonus, autonomic instability
recommendation: NEVER combine. Allow 14-day washout between SSRI and MAOI. 5-week washout for fluoxetine due to long half-life.

effect: Increased risk of serotonin syndrome and seizures
recommendation: Avoid combination if possible. If necessary, use lowest doses and monitor for serotonin syndrome symptoms.

effect: Increased GI bleeding risk — SSRIs affect platelet serotonin
recommendation: Add PPI if NSAID needed. Use acetaminophen as first-line analgesic instead.

effect: Combined AV node suppression — risk of severe bradycardia, heart block, heart failure
recommendation: Avoid combination. If both rate control needed, use with extreme caution and continuous monitoring.

effect: Amiodarone increases digoxin levels by 70-100%, toxicity risk
recommendation: Reduce digoxin dose by 50% when starting amiodarone. Monitor digoxin levels closely.

effect: Furosemide-induced hypokalemia increases digoxin toxicity risk
recommendation: Monitor potassium closely. Supplement potassium if needed. Target K+ 4.0-5.0 mEq/L.

effect: Ciprofloxacin inhibits theophylline metabolism, causing toxicity — seizures, arrhythmias
recommendation: Avoid combination. If essential, reduce theophylline dose by 50% and monitor levels.

effect: Antacids containing aluminum, magnesium, or calcium reduce ciprofloxacin absorption by up to 90%
recommendation: Take ciprofloxacin 2h before or 6h after antacids.

effect: NSAIDs reduce methotrexate renal clearance, increasing toxicity risk — bone marrow suppression
recommendation: Avoid NSAIDs with high-dose methotrexate. With low-dose, use cautiously with monitoring.

effect: Both are folate antagonists — additive bone marrow suppression
recommendation: Avoid combination. If both needed, ensure adequate folate supplementation and close CBC monitoring.

effect: NSAIDs reduce lithium renal excretion, increasing levels by 15-30%
recommendation: Avoid NSAIDs. Use acetaminophen for pain. If NSAID essential, monitor lithium levels within 5 days.

effect: ACE inhibitors reduce lithium excretion, toxicity risk
recommendation: Monitor lithium levels when starting, changing, or stopping ACE inhibitors. Reduce lithium dose if needed.

effect: Omeprazole inhibits CYP2C19, reducing clopidogrel activation by 40-50%
recommendation: Use pantoprazole instead of omeprazole. Pantoprazole has minimal CYP2C19 interaction.

effect: Erythromycin inhibits theophylline metabolism, causing toxicity
recommendation: Reduce theophylline dose by 25% and monitor levels. Consider azithromycin as alternative.

effect: Severe hypotension — potentially fatal. Combined vasodilation
recommendation: NEVER combine. Allow 24h between sildenafil and nitrates (48h for tadalafil).

effect: Hyperkalemia risk from dual potassium retention
recommendation: Monitor potassium within 1 week of starting combination and regularly thereafter.

effect: Fluconazole inhibits CYP2C9, increasing warfarin effect — bleeding risk
recommendation: Monitor INR closely. Reduce warfarin dose by 25-50% during fluconazole treatment.

effect: Carbamazepine induces CYP3A4, reducing contraceptive efficacy — unplanned pregnancy risk
recommendation: Use alternative contraception (IUD, depot injection). If OCP used, use formulation with ≥50mcg ethinyl estradiol.

effect: Complex bidirectional interaction — initially increases then decreases warfarin effect
recommendation: Monitor INR frequently when starting, adjusting, or stopping phenytoin. Dose adjustments likely needed.

effect: Amlodipine increases simvastatin levels via CYP3A4 inhibition — myopathy risk
recommendation: Do not exceed simvastatin 20mg daily with amlodipine. Consider switching to atorvastatin or rosuvastatin.

effect: Amiodarone inhibits warfarin metabolism, increasing INR by 30-50%
recommendation: Reduce warfarin dose by 30-50% when starting amiodarone. Monitor INR weekly for first month.

effect: Hyperkalemia risk from combined potassium retention
recommendation: Monitor potassium within 3 days, then weekly for first month. Avoid in patients with eGFR <30.

effect: Combined QT prolongation — risk of torsades de pointes
recommendation: Avoid combination. Use alternative antibiotic. If unavoidable, continuous ECG monitoring.

effect: Increased GI ulceration and bleeding risk
recommendation: Add PPI prophylaxis if both needed. Monitor for GI symptoms.

effect: Beta-blockers can mask hypoglycemia symptoms (tremor, tachycardia) and prolong hypoglycemia
recommendation: Use cardioselective beta-blockers (metoprolol, bisoprolol). Educate patient on non-masked hypoglycemia symptoms (sweating, hunger).

effect: Calcium reduces levothyroxine absorption
recommendation: Separate administration by at least 4 hours. Take levothyroxine on empty stomach in morning.

effect: Iron reduces levothyroxine absorption by forming insoluble complex
recommendation: Separate administration by at least 4 hours.

effect: Iron chelates ciprofloxacin, reducing absorption by up to 90%
recommendation: Take ciprofloxacin 2h before or 6h after iron supplements.

effect: Combined CNS and respiratory depression — overdose and death risk
recommendation: Avoid combination. If both essential, use lowest doses and shortest duration. Prescribe naloxone for emergency.

effect: Increased hepatotoxicity risk — alcohol induces CYP2E1 which produces toxic metabolite NAPQI
recommendation: Limit acetaminophen to 2g/day in regular alcohol users. Avoid if >3 drinks/day.
